creator: sunpoet@FreeBSD.org
text:
I actually use sqlparse 0.2.2. I could run "import sqlparse" without error for both 0.1.6 and 0.2.0.

from your log:
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/migrate/versioning/script/sql.py", line 7, in <module>
    import sqlparse
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/sqlparse/__init__.py", line 14, in <module>
    from sqlparse import filters
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/sqlparse/filters/__init__.py", line 20, in <module>
    from sqlparse.filters.reindent import ReindentFilter
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/sqlparse/filters/reindent.py", line 10, in <module>
    from sqlparse.utils import offset, indent

Your log shows that you have sqlparse 0.2.0 installed.

Note that there is no /usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/sqlparse/filters/ directory in sqlpare 0.1.6.

% pkg info -x sqlparse
py27-sqlparse-0.1.6
% ls /usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/sqlparse/filters.py*
/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/sqlparse/filters.py   /usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/sqlparse/filters.pyc  /usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/sqlparse/filters.pyo

I guess you have a corrupted sqlparse installation.
